````python
24: class TestNormalizeSourceType:
25:     """Test normalize_source_type function."""
26: 
27:     def test_generic_types(self):
28:         """Generic types normalize correctly."""
29:         assert normalize_source_type("file") == "file"
30:         assert normalize_source_type("url") == "url"
31:         assert normalize_source_type("api") == "api"
32: 
33:     def test_unknown_source(self):
34:         """Unrecognized source types return 'unknown'."""
35:         assert normalize_source_type("random_thing") == "unknown"
36:         assert normalize_source_type("foobar") == "unknown"
37: 
38:     def test_empty_string(self):
39:         """Empty string returns 'unknown'."""
40:         assert normalize_source_type("") == "unknown"
41: 
42:     def test_whitespace_handling(self):
43:         """Leading/trailing whitespace is handled."""
44:         assert normalize_source_type("  file  ") == "file"

46: 
47: class TestRegistration:
48:     """Test dynamic source type registration."""
49: 
50:     def test_register_source_type(self):
51:         """Registering a source type makes it known and normalizable."""
52:         register_source_type("slack", display_name="Slack")
53:         assert normalize_source_type("slack") == "slack"
54:         assert is_known_source("slack")
55: 
56:         # Clean up
57:         SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES.pop("slack", None)
58: 
59:     def test_register_source_alias(self):
60:         """Registering aliases maps them to canonical types."""
61:         register_source_type("linear")
62:         register_source_alias("linear_issue", "linear")
63:         register_source_alias("linear_project", "linear")
64: 
65:         assert normalize_source_type("linear_issue") == "linear"
66:         assert normalize_source_type("linear_project") == "linear"
67: 
68:         # Clean up
69:         SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES.pop("linear", None)
70:         SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES.pop("linear_issue", None)
71:         SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES.pop("linear_project", None)
72: 
73:     def test_register_case_insensitive(self):
74:         """Registration and lookup should be case-insensitive."""
75:         register_source_type("GitHub")
76:         assert normalize_source_type("github") == "github"
77:         assert normalize_source_type("GitHub") == "github"
78: 
79:         # Clean up
80:         SOURCE_TYPE_ALIASES.pop("github", None)
81: 
82:     def test_is_known_source_unregistered(self):
83:         """Unregistered source types should not be known."""
84:         assert not is_known_source("totally_new_tool")
````

## File: tests/unit/test_storage_coordinator.py
````python
  1: """Unit tests for storage/coordinator.py — StorageCoordinator."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
  6: from uuid import uuid4
  7: 
  8: import pytest
  9: 
 10: from khora.core.models import Chunk, Document, Entity, MemoryEvent, Relationship
 11: from khora.storage.coordinator import StorageCoordinator, StorageHealth
 12: 
 13: 
 14: class TestStorageHealth:
 15:     """Tests for StorageHealth dataclass."""
 16: 
 17:     def test_healthy_when_relational_and_vector(self) -> None:
 18:         """is_healthy requires relational and vector."""
 19:         h = StorageHealth(relational=True, vector=True, graph=False, event_store=False)
 20:         assert h.is_healthy is True
 21: 
 22:     def test_unhealthy_without_relational(self) -> None:
 23:         """Missing relational makes it unhealthy."""
 24:         h = StorageHealth(relational=False, vector=True)
 25:         assert h.is_healthy is False
 26: 
 27:     def test_unhealthy_without_vector(self) -> None:
 28:         """Missing vector makes it unhealthy."""
 29:         h = StorageHealth(relational=True, vector=False)
 30:         assert h.is_healthy is False
 31: 
 32:     def test_summary(self) -> None:
 33:         """summary returns dict of all backends."""
 34:         h = StorageHealth(relational=True, vector=True, graph=True, event_store=False)
 35:         summary = h.summary
 36:         assert summary == {
 37:             "relational": True,
 38:             "vector": True,
 39:             "graph": True,
 40:             "event_store": False,
 41:         }
 42: 
 43: 
 44: class TestStorageCoordinatorLifecycle:
 45:     """Tests for connect/disconnect lifecycle."""
 46: 
 47:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 48:     async def test_connect(self) -> None:
 49:         """Connect calls connect on all backends."""
 50:         rel = MagicMock()
 51:         rel.connect = AsyncMock()
 52:         vec = MagicMock()
 53:         vec.connect = AsyncMock()
 54:         graph = MagicMock()
 55:         graph.connect = AsyncMock()
 56: 
 57:         coord = StorageCoordinator(relational=rel, vector=vec, graph=graph)
 58:         await coord.connect()
 59: 
 60:         rel.connect.assert_awaited_once()
 61:         vec.connect.assert_awaited_once()
 62:         graph.connect.assert_awaited_once()
 63:         assert coord._connected is True
 64: 
 65:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 66:     async def test_connect_idempotent(self) -> None:
 67:         """Second connect call is a no-op."""
 68:         rel = MagicMock()
 69:         rel.connect = AsyncMock()
 70:         coord = StorageCoordinator(relational=rel)
 71:         await coord.connect()
 72:         await coord.connect()
 73:         rel.connect.assert_awaited_once()
 74: 
 75:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 76:     async def test_disconnect(self) -> None:
 77:         """Disconnect calls disconnect on all backends in reverse order."""
 78:         rel = MagicMock()
 79:         rel.connect = AsyncMock()
 80:         rel.disconnect = AsyncMock()
 81:         vec = MagicMock()
 82:         vec.connect = AsyncMock()
 83:         vec.disconnect = AsyncMock()
 84: 
 85:         coord = StorageCoordinator(relational=rel, vector=vec)
 86:         await coord.connect()
 87:         await coord.disconnect()
 88: 
 89:         rel.disconnect.assert_awaited_once()
 90:         vec.disconnect.assert_awaited_once()
 91:         assert coord._connected is False
 92: 
 93:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 94:     async def test_disconnect_when_not_connected(self) -> None:
 95:         """Disconnect is no-op when not connected."""
 96:         rel = MagicMock()
 97:         rel.disconnect = AsyncMock()
 98:         coord = StorageCoordinator(relational=rel)
 99:         await coord.disconnect()
100:         rel.disconnect.assert_not_awaited()
101: 
102:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
103:     async def test_health_check(self) -> None:
104:         """Health check queries all backends."""
105:         rel = MagicMock()
106:         rel.is_healthy = AsyncMock(return_value=True)
107:         vec = MagicMock()
108:         vec.is_healthy = AsyncMock(return_value=True)
109:         graph = MagicMock()
110:         graph.is_healthy = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
111: 
112:         coord = StorageCoordinator(relational=rel, vector=vec, graph=graph)
113:         health = await coord.health_check()
114: 
115:         assert health.relational is True
116:         assert health.vector is True
117:         assert health.graph is False
````

## File: tests/unit/test_telemetry_collector.py
````python
  1: """Tests for telemetry collector and initialization."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
  6: from uuid import uuid4
  7: 
  8: import pytest
  9: 
 10: from khora.telemetry import NoOpCollector, TelemetryCollector, get_collector, init_telemetry, shutdown_telemetry
 11: from khora.telemetry.config import TelemetryConfig
 12: from khora.telemetry.models import LLMEvent, PipelineEvent, StorageEvent
 13: 
 14: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 15: # Models
 16: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 17: 
 18: 
 19: class TestModels:
 20:     def test_llm_event_defaults(self):
 21:         event = LLMEvent()
 22:         assert event.service_name == "khora"
 23:         assert event.status == "success"
 24:         assert event.prompt_tokens == 0
 25: 
 26:     def test_storage_event_defaults(self):
 27:         event = StorageEvent()
 28:         assert event.backend == ""
 29:         assert event.record_count == 0
 30: 
 31:     def test_pipeline_event_defaults(self):
 32:         event = PipelineEvent()
 33:         assert event.pipeline == ""
 34:         assert event.run_id is None
 35: 
 36:     def test_llm_event_with_values(self):
 37:         event = LLMEvent(
 38:             operation="embedding",
 39:             model="text-embedding-3-small",
 40:             prompt_tokens=100,
 41:             total_tokens=100,
 42:             latency_ms=50.0,
 43:         )
 44:         assert event.model == "text-embedding-3-small"
 45:         assert event.prompt_tokens == 100
 46: 
 47: 
 48: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 49: # Config
 50: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 51: 
 52: 
 53: class TestTelemetryConfig:
 54:     def test_from_env_defaults(self):
 55:         with patch.dict("os.environ", {}, clear=True):
 56:             cfg = TelemetryConfig.from_env()
 57:             assert cfg.database_url is None
 58:             assert cfg.service_name == "khora"
 59: 
 60:     def test_from_env_with_url(self):
 61:         with patch.dict("os.environ", {"KHORA_TELEMETRY_DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://localhost/telemetry"}):
 62:             cfg = TelemetryConfig.from_env()
 63:             assert cfg.database_url == "postgresql://localhost/telemetry"
 64: 
 65:     def test_from_env_custom_service(self):
 66:         with patch.dict("os.environ", {"KHORA_TELEMETRY_SERVICE_NAME": "genesis"}):
 67:             cfg = TelemetryConfig.from_env()
 68:             assert cfg.service_name == "genesis"
 69: 
 70: 
 71: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 72: # NoOpCollector
 73: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 74: 
 75: 
 76: class TestNoOpCollector:
 77:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 78:     async def test_noop_start_shutdown(self):
 79:         collector = NoOpCollector()
 80:         await collector.start()
 81:         await collector.shutdown()
 82: 
 83:     def test_noop_record_methods(self):
 84:         collector = NoOpCollector()
 85:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="test", model="gpt-4o")
 86:         collector.record_storage_op(backend="postgresql", operation="create_document")
 87:         collector.record_pipeline_stage(pipeline="ingestion", stage="chunking")
 88:         # Should not raise
 89: 
 90: 
 91: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 92: # TelemetryCollector
 93: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 94: 
 95: 
 96: class TestTelemetryCollector:
 97:     def test_record_llm_call_buffers(self):
 98:         engine = MagicMock()
 99:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
100:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="embedding", model="gpt-4o", prompt_tokens=100)
101:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 1
102:         kind, data = collector._buffer[0]
103:         assert kind == "llm"
104:         assert data["operation"] == "embedding"
105:         assert data["model"] == "gpt-4o"
106: 
107:     def test_record_storage_op_buffers(self):
108:         engine = MagicMock()
109:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
110:         collector.record_storage_op(backend="pgvector", operation="search_similar_chunks", record_count=10)
111:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 1
112:         kind, data = collector._buffer[0]
113:         assert kind == "storage"
114:         assert data["backend"] == "pgvector"
115:         assert data["record_count"] == 10
116: 
117:     def test_record_pipeline_stage_buffers(self):
118:         engine = MagicMock()
119:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
120:         run_id = uuid4()
121:         collector.record_pipeline_stage(pipeline="ingestion", stage="chunking", run_id=run_id)
122:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 1
123:         kind, data = collector._buffer[0]
124:         assert kind == "pipeline"
125:         assert data["pipeline"] == "ingestion"
126: 
127:     def test_service_name_applied(self):
128:         engine = MagicMock()
129:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="genesis")
130:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="chat")
131:         _, data = collector._buffer[0]
132:         assert data["service_name"] == "genesis"
133: 
134:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
135:     async def test_flush_drains_buffer(self):
136:         engine = AsyncMock()
137:         conn = AsyncMock()
138:         engine.begin.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=conn)
139:         engine.begin.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
140: 
141:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
142:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="test")
143:         collector.record_storage_op(backend="pg", operation="test")
144:         collector.record_pipeline_stage(pipeline="test", stage="test")
145: 
146:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 3
147:         await collector._flush()
148:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 0
149: 
150:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
151:     async def test_flush_handles_errors_gracefully(self):
152:         engine = AsyncMock()
153:         engine.begin.side_effect = Exception("connection failed")
154: 
155:         collector = TelemetryCollector(engine, service_name="test")
156:         collector.record_llm_call(operation="test")
157: 
158:         # Should not raise
159:         await collector._flush()
160:         # Buffer is drained even on error (events are dropped)
161:         assert len(collector._buffer) == 0
162: 
163: 
164: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
165: # init_telemetry / shutdown_telemetry
166: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
167: 
168: 
169: class TestInitTelemetry:
170:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
171:     async def test_init_returns_noop_when_no_url(self):
172:         cfg = TelemetryConfig(database_url=None)
173:         collector = await init_telemetry(cfg)
174:         assert isinstance(collector, NoOpCollector)
175:         assert isinstance(get_collector(), NoOpCollector)
176: 
177:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
178:     async def test_init_returns_collector_when_url_set(self):
179:         cfg = TelemetryConfig(database_url="postgresql://localhost/telemetry")
180: 
181:         with patch("khora.telemetry.session.create_telemetry_engine") as mock_engine_factory:
182:             mock_engine = MagicMock()
183:             # Make begin() return an async context manager
184:             mock_conn = AsyncMock()
185:             cm = AsyncMock()
186:             cm.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_conn)
187:             cm.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(return_value=False)
188:             mock_engine.begin.return_value = cm
189:             mock_engine.dispose = AsyncMock()
190:             mock_engine_factory.return_value = mock_engine
191: 
192:             collector = await init_telemetry(cfg)
193:             assert isinstance(collector, TelemetryCollector)
194:             assert isinstance(get_collector(), TelemetryCollector)
195: 
196:             # Clean up
197:             await shutdown_telemetry()
198:             assert isinstance(get_collector(), NoOpCollector)
199: 
200:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
201:     async def test_init_from_env_defaults(self):
202:         with patch.dict("os.environ", {}, clear=True):
203:             collector = await init_telemetry()
204:             assert isinstance(collector, NoOpCollector)
````

## File: tests/unit/test_telemetry_instrument.py
````python
  1: """Tests for telemetry instrumentation helpers."""
  2: 
  3: from __future__ import annotations
  4: 
  5: import asyncio
  6: from unittest.mock import patch
  7: from uuid import uuid4
  8: 
  9: import pytest
 10: 
 11: from khora.telemetry import NoOpCollector
 12: from khora.telemetry.instrument import instrument_llm, instrument_storage, pipeline_stage
 13: 
 14: 
 15: class _RecordingCollector(NoOpCollector):
 16:     """Collector that records calls for assertions."""
 17: 
 18:     def __init__(self):
 19:         self.llm_calls: list[dict] = []
 20:         self.storage_calls: list[dict] = []
 21:         self.pipeline_calls: list[dict] = []
 22: 
 23:     def record_llm_call(self, **kwargs):
 24:         self.llm_calls.append(kwargs)
 25: 
 26:     def record_storage_op(self, **kwargs):
 27:         self.storage_calls.append(kwargs)
 28: 
 29:     def record_pipeline_stage(self, **kwargs):
 30:         self.pipeline_calls.append(kwargs)
 31: 
 32: 
 33: @pytest.fixture
 34: def recording_collector():
 35:     collector = _RecordingCollector()
 36:     with patch("khora.telemetry._collector", collector):
 37:         yield collector
 38: 
 39: 
 40: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 41: # @instrument_llm
 42: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 43: 
 44: 
 45: class TestInstrumentLLM:
 46:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 47:     async def test_records_success(self, recording_collector):
 48:         @instrument_llm("test_op")
 49:         async def my_llm_call():
 50:             return "result"
 51: 
 52:         result = await my_llm_call()
 53:         assert result == "result"
 54:         assert len(recording_collector.llm_calls) == 1
 55:         call = recording_collector.llm_calls[0]
 56:         assert call["operation"] == "test_op"
 57:         assert call["status"] == "success"
 58:         assert call["latency_ms"] > 0
 59:         assert call["error_message"] is None
 60: 
 61:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 62:     async def test_records_error(self, recording_collector):
 63:         @instrument_llm("failing_op")
 64:         async def my_llm_call():
 65:             raise ValueError("boom")
 66: 
 67:         with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="boom"):
 68:             await my_llm_call()
 69: 
 70:         assert len(recording_collector.llm_calls) == 1
 71:         call = recording_collector.llm_calls[0]
 72:         assert call["status"] == "error"
 73:         assert "boom" in call["error_message"]
 74: 
 75: 
 76: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 77: # @instrument_storage
 78: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 79: 
 80: 
 81: class TestInstrumentStorage:
 82:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 83:     async def test_records_success_with_list_result(self, recording_collector):
 84:         @instrument_storage("pgvector", "search_similar")
 85:         async def search():
 86:             return [1, 2, 3]
 87: 
 88:         result = await search()
 89:         assert result == [1, 2, 3]
 90:         assert len(recording_collector.storage_calls) == 1
 91:         call = recording_collector.storage_calls[0]
 92:         assert call["backend"] == "pgvector"
 93:         assert call["operation"] == "search_similar"
 94:         assert call["record_count"] == 3
 95:         assert call["status"] == "success"
 96: 
 97:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
 98:     async def test_records_single_result(self, recording_collector):
 99:         @instrument_storage("postgresql", "create_document")
100:         async def create():
101:             return {"id": 1}
102: 
103:         await create()
104:         call = recording_collector.storage_calls[0]
105:         assert call["record_count"] == 1
106: 
107:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
108:     async def test_records_error(self, recording_collector):
109:         @instrument_storage("neo4j", "create_entity")
110:         async def failing():
111:             raise ConnectionError("db down")
112: 
113:         with pytest.raises(ConnectionError):
114:             await failing()
115: 
116:         call = recording_collector.storage_calls[0]
117:         assert call["status"] == "error"
118:         assert "db down" in call["error_message"]
119: 
120: 
121: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
122: # pipeline_stage context manager
123: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
124: 
125: 
126: class TestPipelineStage:
127:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
128:     async def test_records_success(self, recording_collector):
129:         run_id = uuid4()
130:         ns_id = uuid4()
131:         async with pipeline_stage("ingestion", "chunking", run_id, namespace_id=ns_id):
132:             await asyncio.sleep(0.001)
133: 
134:         assert len(recording_collector.pipeline_calls) == 1
135:         call = recording_collector.pipeline_calls[0]
136:         assert call["pipeline"] == "ingestion"
137:         assert call["stage"] == "chunking"
138:         assert call["run_id"] == run_id
139:         assert call["namespace_id"] == ns_id
140:         assert call["status"] == "success"
141:         assert call["latency_ms"] > 0
142: 
143:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
144:     async def test_records_error(self, recording_collector):
145:         with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="stage failed"):
146:             async with pipeline_stage("query", "understanding", uuid4()):
147:                 raise RuntimeError("stage failed")
148: 
149:         call = recording_collector.pipeline_calls[0]
150:         assert call["status"] == "error"
151:         assert "stage failed" in call["error_message"]
152: 
153:     @pytest.mark.asyncio
154:     async def test_extra_metadata(self, recording_collector):
155:         async with pipeline_stage("ingestion", "embedding", uuid4(), extra_metadata={"chunk_count": 42}):
156:             pass
157: 
158:         call = recording_collector.pipeline_calls[0]
159:         assert call["metadata"] == {"chunk_count": 42}
````

## File: src/khora/config/llm.py
````python
  1: """LiteLLM configuration for unified LLM access.
  2: 
  3: Provides a unified interface to all LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.)
  4: with fallbacks and routing. Based on the memoryman/potemkin pattern.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import os
 10: from pathlib import Path
 11: from typing import Any
 12: 
 13: import yaml
 14: from loguru import logger
 15: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
 16: 
 17: 
 18: class LiteLLMConfig(BaseModel):
 19:     """Configuration for LiteLLM unified model access.
 20: 
 21:     This configuration provides a unified interface to all LLM providers
 22:     including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and others through LiteLLM.
 23:     """
 24: 
 25:     # Primary model configuration
 26:     model: str = Field(
 27:         default="gpt-4o-mini",
 28:         description="Primary model to use (e.g., gpt-4o-mini, claude-sonnet-4-20250514, gemini-2.0-flash)",
 29:     )
 30:     api_key_env: str = Field(
 31:         default="OPENAI_API_KEY",
 32:         description="Environment variable name for API key",
 33:     )
 34: 
 35:     # Model parameters
 36:     temperature: float = Field(
 37:         default=0.7,
 38:         ge=0.0,
 39:         le=2.0,
 40:         description="Sampling temperature for generation",
 41:     )
 42:     max_tokens: int = Field(
 43:         default=2000,
 44:         gt=0,
 45:         description="Maximum tokens to generate",
 46:     )
 47: 
 48:     # Request configuration
 49:     timeout: int = Field(
 50:         default=30,
 51:         gt=0,
 52:         description="Request timeout in seconds",
 53:     )
 54:     max_retries: int = Field(
 55:         default=3,
 56:         ge=0,
 57:         description="Maximum number of retries on failure",
 58:     )
 59:     retry_wait: int = Field(
 60:         default=2,
 61:         ge=0,
 62:         description="Wait time between retries in seconds",
 63:     )
 64: 
 65:     # Concurrency
 66:     max_concurrent_llm_calls: int = Field(
 67:         default=10,
 68:         gt=0,
 69:         description="Maximum concurrent LLM API calls",
 70:     )
 71: 
 72:     # Router configuration for fallbacks
 73:     model_list: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = Field(
 74:         default=None,
 75:         description="List of model configurations for router fallbacks",
 76:     )
 77:     router_settings: dict[str, Any] | None = Field(
 78:         default=None,
 79:         description="Router settings (routing_strategy, num_retries, etc.)",
 80:     )
 81: 
 82:     # Embedding model configuration
 83:     embedding_model: str = Field(
 84:         default="text-embedding-3-small",
 85:         description="Model to use for embeddings",
 86:     )
 87:     embedding_api_key_env: str | None = Field(
 88:         default=None,
 89:         description="Environment variable for embedding API key (defaults to api_key_env)",
 90:     )
 91:     embedding_dimension: int = Field(
 92:         default=1536,
 93:         gt=0,
 94:         description="Embedding vector dimension",
 95:     )
 96:     embed_concurrency: int = Field(
 97:         default=10,
 98:         gt=0,
 99:         description="Maximum concurrent embedding API calls",
100:     )
101: 
102:     @classmethod
103:     def from_yaml(cls, path: str | Path) -> LiteLLMConfig:
104:         """Load configuration from a YAML file.
105: 
106:         Args:
107:             path: Path to the YAML configuration file
108: 
109:         Returns:
110:             LiteLLMConfig instance
111:         """
112:         path = Path(path)
113:         with path.open() as f:
114:             data = yaml.safe_load(f)
115:         return cls.model_validate(data or {})
116: 
117:     @classmethod
118:     def from_dict(cls, config: dict[str, Any]) -> LiteLLMConfig:
119:         """Create configuration from a dictionary.
120: 
121:         Args:
122:             config: Configuration dictionary (can be nested under 'llm' key)
123: 
124:         Returns:
125:             LiteLLMConfig instance
126:         """
127:         # Handle nested configuration
128:         if "llm" in config:
129:             config = config["llm"]
130: 
131:         # Handle config_file path
132:         if "config_file" in config:
133:             return cls.from_yaml(config["config_file"])
134: 
135:         return cls.model_validate(config)
136: 
137:     def get_api_key(self) -> str:
138:         """Get the API key from environment variable."""
139:         key = os.environ.get(self.api_key_env, "")
140:         if not key:
141:             logger.warning(f"API key environment variable {self.api_key_env} not set")
142:         return key
143: 
144:     def get_embedding_api_key(self) -> str:
145:         """Get the embedding API key from environment variable."""
146:         env_var = self.embedding_api_key_env or self.api_key_env
147:         key = os.environ.get(env_var, "")
148:         if not key:
149:             logger.warning(f"Embedding API key environment variable {env_var} not set")
150:         return key
151: 
152: 
153: def configure_litellm(config: LiteLLMConfig | None = None) -> None:
154:     """Configure LiteLLM with the given configuration.
155: 
156:     This function should be called once at application startup to configure
157:     LiteLLM's global settings.
158: 
159:     Args:
160:         config: LiteLLM configuration (uses defaults if None)
161:     """
162:     try:
163:         import litellm
164:     except ImportError:
165:         logger.warning("litellm package not installed, skipping configuration")
166:         return
167: 
168:     if config is None:
169:         config = LiteLLMConfig()
170: 
171:     # Critical for compatibility across providers
172:     litellm.drop_params = True
173: 
174:     # Disable verbose logging and telemetry
175:     litellm.set_verbose = False
176:     litellm.telemetry = False
177: 
178:     # Set up API keys from environment
179:     api_key = config.get_api_key()
180:     if api_key:
181:         # LiteLLM uses provider-specific env vars, so we ensure they're set
182:         if "openai" in config.model.lower() or config.model.startswith("gpt"):
183:             os.environ.setdefault("OPENAI_API_KEY", api_key)
184:         elif "claude" in config.model.lower() or "anthropic" in config.model.lower():
185:             os.environ.setdefault("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", api_key)
186:         elif "gemini" in config.model.lower():
187:             os.environ.setdefault("GOOGLE_API_KEY", api_key)
188: 
189:     logger.info(f"LiteLLM configured with model: {config.model}")
190: 
191: 
192: def create_litellm_router(config: LiteLLMConfig) -> Any:
193:     """Create a LiteLLM router for fallback handling.
194: 
195:     Args:
196:         config: LiteLLM configuration with model_list
197: 
198:     Returns:
199:         LiteLLM Router instance
200:     """
201:     try:
202:         from litellm import Router
203:     except ImportError:
204:         logger.warning("litellm package not installed")
205:         return None
206: 
207:     if not config.model_list:
208:         logger.warning("No model_list configured, router not created")
209:         return None
210: 
211:     router_settings = config.router_settings or {
212:         "routing_strategy": "simple-shuffle",
213:         "num_retries": config.max_retries,
214:     }
215: 
216:     router = Router(
217:         model_list=config.model_list,
218:         **router_settings,
219:     )
220: 
221:     logger.info(f"LiteLLM router created with {len(config.model_list)} models")
222:     return router
223: 
224: 
225: async def acompletion(
226:     prompt: str,
227:     config: LiteLLMConfig | None = None,
228:     *,
229:     system_prompt: str | None = None,
230:     **kwargs: Any,
231: ) -> str:
232:     """Async completion with LiteLLM.
233: 
234:     Args:
235:         prompt: User prompt
236:         config: LiteLLM configuration (uses defaults if None)
237:         system_prompt: Optional system prompt
238:         **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to litellm.acompletion
239: 
240:     Returns:
241:         Generated text response
242:     """
243:     try:
244:         import litellm
245:     except ImportError:
246:         raise RuntimeError("litellm package not installed. Run: pip install litellm")
247: 
248:     if config is None:
249:         config = LiteLLMConfig()
250: 
251:     messages = []
252:     if system_prompt:
253:         messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system_prompt})
254:     messages.append({"role": "user", "content": prompt})
255: 
256:     import time as _time
257: 
258:     _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
259:     response = await litellm.acompletion(
260:         model=config.model,
261:         messages=messages,
262:         temperature=kwargs.pop("temperature", config.temperature),
263:         max_tokens=kwargs.pop("max_tokens", config.max_tokens),
264:         timeout=kwargs.pop("timeout", config.timeout),
265:         num_retries=kwargs.pop("num_retries", config.max_retries),
266:         **kwargs,
267:     )
268:     _latency = (_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000
269: 
270:     # Record telemetry
271:     from khora.telemetry import get_collector
272: 
273:     usage = getattr(response, "usage", None)
274:     get_collector().record_llm_call(
275:         operation=kwargs.get("_telemetry_op", "completion"),
276:         model=config.model,
277:         prompt_tokens=getattr(usage, "prompt_tokens", 0) or 0,
278:         completion_tokens=getattr(usage, "completion_tokens", 0) or 0,
279:         total_tokens=getattr(usage, "total_tokens", 0) or 0,
280:         latency_ms=_latency,
281:     )
282: 
283:     return response.choices[0].message.content
284: 
285: 
286: async def aembedding(
287:     text: str | list[str],
288:     config: LiteLLMConfig | None = None,
289:     **kwargs: Any,
290: ) -> list[list[float]]:
291:     """Async embedding generation with LiteLLM.
292: 
293:     Args:
294:         text: Text or list of texts to embed
295:         config: LiteLLM configuration (uses defaults if None)
296:         **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to litellm.aembedding
297: 
298:     Returns:
299:         List of embedding vectors
300:     """
301:     try:
302:         import litellm
303:     except ImportError:
304:         raise RuntimeError("litellm package not installed. Run: pip install litellm")
305: 
306:     if config is None:
307:         config = LiteLLMConfig()
308: 
309:     # Ensure text is a list
310:     if isinstance(text, str):
311:         text = [text]
312: 
313:     import time as _time
314: 
315:     _t0 = _time.perf_counter()
316:     response = await litellm.aembedding(
317:         model=config.embedding_model,
318:         input=text,
319:         timeout=kwargs.pop("timeout", config.timeout),
320:         **kwargs,
321:     )
322:     _latency = (_time.perf_counter() - _t0) * 1000
323: 
324:     # Record telemetry
325:     from khora.telemetry import get_collector
326: 
327:     usage = getattr(response, "usage", None)
328:     get_collector().record_llm_call(
329:         operation="embedding",
330:         model=config.embedding_model,
331:         prompt_tokens=getattr(usage, "prompt_tokens", 0) or 0,
332:         total_tokens=getattr(usage, "total_tokens", 0) or 0,
333:         latency_ms=_latency,
334:         metadata={"batch_size": len(text)},
335:     )
336: 
337:     return [item["embedding"] for item in response.data]
````

## File: src/khora/extraction/expansion/cross_tool_unifier.py
````python
  1: """Cross-tool entity unification.
  2: 
  3: Unifies entities from different tools/sources that represent the same
  4: real-world concept using correlation rules from expertise configuration.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 10: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 11: from uuid import UUID
 12: 
 13: from loguru import logger
 14: 
 15: from khora._accel import cosine_similarity, levenshtein_similarity
 16: 
 17: from .rule_engine import RuleEngine, RuleEvaluationContext
 18: 
 19: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 20:     from khora.core.models import Entity, Relationship
 21:     from khora.extraction.skills import ExpertiseConfig
 22: 
 23:     from .entity_index import EntityIndex
 24: 
 25: 
 26: @dataclass
 27: class UnificationResult:
 28:     """Result of cross-tool entity unification."""
 29: 
 30:     # Entities after unification (merged duplicates)
 31:     unified_entities: list[Entity] = field(default_factory=list)
 32: 
 33:     # Mapping from original entity IDs to unified entity IDs
 34:     entity_mapping: dict[UUID, UUID] = field(default_factory=dict)
 35: 
 36:     # Relationships updated with unified entity references
 37:     updated_relationships: list[Relationship] = field(default_factory=list)
 38: 
 39:     # New relationships created by correlation rules
 40:     new_relationships: list[Relationship] = field(default_factory=list)
 41: 
 42:     # Statistics
 43:     entities_merged: int = 0
 44:     merge_groups: list[list[UUID]] = field(default_factory=list)  # Groups of merged entity IDs
 45: 
 46: 
 47: class CrossToolUnifier:
 48:     """Unifies entities across different tools and sources.
 49: 
 50:     Uses correlation rules from expertise configuration to identify
 51:     entities that should be merged:
 52:     - Email matching for people
 53:     - Domain matching for companies
 54:     - Pattern matching for references (e.g., JIRA-123)
 55:     - Embedding similarity for fuzzy matching
 56:     """
 57: 
 58:     def __init__(
 59:         self,
 60:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig | None = None,
 61:         *,
 62:         embedding_threshold: float = 0.85,
 63:         fuzzy_threshold: float = 0.85,
 64:     ) -> None:
 65:         """Initialize the cross-tool unifier.
 66: 
 67:         Args:
 68:             expertise: ExpertiseConfig with correlation rules
 69:             embedding_threshold: Similarity threshold for embedding matching
 70:             fuzzy_threshold: Threshold for fuzzy string matching
 71:         """
 72:         self._expertise = expertise
 73:         self._embedding_threshold = embedding_threshold
 74:         self._fuzzy_threshold = fuzzy_threshold
 75:         self._rule_engine = RuleEngine(expertise)
 76: 
 77:     def unify(
 78:         self,
 79:         entities: list[Entity],
 80:         relationships: list[Relationship],
 81:         *,
 82:         use_embeddings: bool = True,
 83:         use_fuzzy: bool = True,
 84:         entity_index: EntityIndex | None = None,
 85:     ) -> UnificationResult:
 86:         """Unify entities and update relationships.
 87: 
 88:         Args:
 89:             entities: Entities to unify
 90:             relationships: Relationships between entities
 91:             use_embeddings: Whether to use embedding similarity
 92:             use_fuzzy: Whether to use fuzzy string matching
 93: 
 94:         Returns:
 95:             UnificationResult with unified entities and updated relationships
 96:         """
 97:         result = UnificationResult()
 98: 
 99:         if not entities:
100:             return result
101: 
102:         # Build evaluation context
103:         context = RuleEvaluationContext.from_data(entities, relationships)
104: 
105:         # Find entity groups that should be merged
106:         merge_groups = self._find_merge_groups(entities, context, use_embeddings, use_fuzzy, entity_index=entity_index)
107: 
108:         if not merge_groups:
109:             # No merging needed
110:             result.unified_entities = entities.copy()
111:             result.updated_relationships = relationships.copy()
112:             return result
113: 
114:         # Merge entities in each group
115:         entity_mapping: dict[UUID, UUID] = {}
116:         unified_entities: list[Entity] = []
117:         processed_ids: set[UUID] = set()
118: 
119:         for group in merge_groups:
120:             if len(group) < 2:
121:                 continue
122: 
123:             # Merge all entities in the group
124:             merged_entity = self._merge_entity_group([e for e in entities if e.id in group])
125:             unified_entities.append(merged_entity)
126: 
127:             # Map all original IDs to the merged entity's ID
128:             for entity_id in group:
129:                 entity_mapping[entity_id] = merged_entity.id
130:                 processed_ids.add(entity_id)
131: 
132:             result.entities_merged += len(group) - 1
133:             result.merge_groups.append(list(group))
134: 
135:         # Add entities that weren't merged
136:         for entity in entities:
137:             if entity.id not in processed_ids:
138:                 unified_entities.append(entity)
139:                 entity_mapping[entity.id] = entity.id
140: 
141:         result.unified_entities = unified_entities
142:         result.entity_mapping = entity_mapping
143: 
144:         # Update relationships with new entity IDs
145:         result.updated_relationships = self._update_relationships(relationships, entity_mapping)
146: 
147:         # Find new relationships from correlation rules
148:         result.new_relationships = self._find_new_relationships(merge_groups, entities)
149: 
150:         logger.debug(
151:             f"Unified {len(entities)} entities into {len(unified_entities)} "
152:             f"({result.entities_merged} merged in {len(merge_groups)} groups)"
153:         )
154: 
155:         return result
156: 
157:     def _find_merge_groups(
158:         self,
159:         entities: list[Entity],
160:         context: RuleEvaluationContext,
161:         use_embeddings: bool,
162:         use_fuzzy: bool,
163:         *,
164:         entity_index: EntityIndex | None = None,
165:     ) -> list[set[UUID]]:
166:         """Find groups of entities that should be merged."""
167:         # Use Union-Find to track merge groups
168:         parent: dict[UUID, UUID] = {e.id: e.id for e in entities}
169: 
170:         def find(x: UUID) -> UUID:
171:             if parent[x] != x:
172:                 parent[x] = find(parent[x])
173:             return parent[x]
174: 
175:         def union(x: UUID, y: UUID) -> None:
176:             px, py = find(x), find(y)
177:             if px != py:
178:                 parent[px] = py
179: 
180:         # Apply correlation rules
181:         if self._expertise and self._expertise.correlation_rules:
182:             for rule in self._expertise.correlation_rules:
183:                 if rule.match_fields:
184:                     matches = self._find_field_match_pairs(entities, rule.match_fields, rule.entity_types)
185:                     for e1_id, e2_id in matches:
186:                         union(e1_id, e2_id)
187: 
188:         # Exact name matching within same type
189:         self._find_exact_name_matches(entities, union)
190: 
191:         # Optional: Embedding similarity matching
192:         if use_embeddings:
193:             embedding_matches = self._find_embedding_matches(entities, entity_index=entity_index)
194:             for e1_id, e2_id in embedding_matches:
195:                 union(e1_id, e2_id)
196: 
197:         # Optional: Fuzzy string matching
198:         if use_fuzzy:
199:             fuzzy_matches = self._find_fuzzy_matches(entities, entity_index=entity_index)
200:             for e1_id, e2_id in fuzzy_matches:
201:                 union(e1_id, e2_id)
202: 
203:         # Build groups from union-find
204:         groups: dict[UUID, set[UUID]] = {}
205:         for entity in entities:
206:             root = find(entity.id)
207:             if root not in groups:
208:                 groups[root] = set()
209:             groups[root].add(entity.id)
210: 
211:         # Return only groups with multiple entities
212:         return [group for group in groups.values() if len(group) > 1]
213: 
214:     def _find_field_match_pairs(
215:         self,
216:         entities: list[Entity],
217:         match_fields: list[str],
218:         entity_types: list[str],
219:     ) -> list[tuple[UUID, UUID]]:
220:         """Find entity pairs that match on specified fields."""
221:         pairs = []
222: 
223:         # Filter by entity types if specified
224:         candidates = entities
225:         if entity_types:
226:             candidates = [
227:                 e
228:                 for e in entities
229:                 if str(e.entity_type.value if hasattr(e.entity_type, "value") else e.entity_type) in entity_types
230:             ]
231: 
232:         # Group by field values
233:         for field_name in match_fields:
234:             field_groups: dict[Any, list[Entity]] = {}
235:             for entity in candidates:
236:                 field_value = entity.attributes.get(field_name)
237:                 if field_value:
238:                     # Normalize strings
239:                     if isinstance(field_value, str):
240:                         field_value = field_value.lower().strip()
241:                     if field_value not in field_groups:
242:                         field_groups[field_value] = []
243:                     field_groups[field_value].append(entity)
244: 
245:             # Create pairs from groups
246:             for group_entities in field_groups.values():
247:                 if len(group_entities) > 1:
248:                     # Add pairs for all combinations in group
249:                     for i, e1 in enumerate(group_entities):
250:                         for e2 in group_entities[i + 1 :]:
251:                             pairs.append((e1.id, e2.id))
252: 
253:         return pairs
254: 
255:     def _find_exact_name_matches(
256:         self,
257:         entities: list[Entity],
258:         union: Any,
259:     ) -> None:
260:         """Find entities with exact name matches (same type)."""
261:         # Group by (normalized_name, type)
262:         name_type_groups: dict[tuple[str, str], list[Entity]] = {}
263:         for entity in entities:
264:             key = (
265:                 entity.name.lower().strip(),
266:                 str(entity.entity_type.value if hasattr(entity.entity_type, "value") else entity.entity_type),
267:             )
268:             if key not in name_type_groups:
269:                 name_type_groups[key] = []
270:             name_type_groups[key].append(entity)
271: 
272:         # Union entities in each group
273:         for group in name_type_groups.values():
274:             if len(group) > 1:
275:                 first = group[0]
276:                 for entity in group[1:]:
277:                     union(first.id, entity.id)
278: 
279:     def _find_embedding_matches(
280:         self,
281:         entities: list[Entity],
282:         *,
283:         entity_index: EntityIndex | None = None,
284:     ) -> list[tuple[UUID, UUID]]:
285:         """Find entity pairs with similar embeddings.
286: 
287:         When *entity_index* is provided, uses token blocking to reduce the
288:         candidate set from O(n^2) to O(n*k) where k is ~10-20 candidates
289:         per entity.
290:         """
291:         pairs = []
292: 
293:         # Filter to entities with embeddings
294:         with_embeddings = [e for e in entities if e.embedding]
295:         if len(with_embeddings) < 2:
296:             return pairs
297: 
298:         if entity_index is not None:
299:             # Blocked matching: O(n*k) via entity_index
300:             seen: set[tuple[UUID, UUID]] = set()
301:             for entity in with_embeddings:
302:                 for candidate, similarity in entity_index.find_embedding_candidates(
303:                     entity, threshold=self._embedding_threshold
304:                 ):
305:                     pair = (min(entity.id, candidate.id), max(entity.id, candidate.id))
306:                     if pair not in seen:
307:                         seen.add(pair)
308:                         pairs.append((entity.id, candidate.id))
309:             return pairs
310: 
311:         # Fallback: O(n^2) pairwise comparison
312:         for i, e1 in enumerate(with_embeddings):
313:             for e2 in with_embeddings[i + 1 :]:
314:                 # Only compare same type
315:                 if e1.entity_type != e2.entity_type:
316:                     continue
317: 
318:                 similarity = cosine_similarity(e1.embedding, e2.embedding)
319:                 if similarity >= self._embedding_threshold:
320:                     pairs.append((e1.id, e2.id))
321: 
322:         return pairs
323: 
324:     def _find_fuzzy_matches(
325:         self,
326:         entities: list[Entity],
327:         *,
328:         entity_index: EntityIndex | None = None,
329:     ) -> list[tuple[UUID, UUID]]:
330:         """Find entity pairs with similar names (fuzzy matching).
331: 
332:         When *entity_index* is provided, uses token blocking to reduce the
333:         candidate set from O(n^2) to O(n*k).
334:         """
335:         pairs = []
336: 
337:         if entity_index is not None:
338:             # Blocked matching: O(n*k)
339:             seen: set[tuple[UUID, UUID]] = set()
340:             for entity in entities:
341:                 for candidate, similarity in entity_index.find_fuzzy_candidates(
342:                     entity, threshold=self._fuzzy_threshold
343:                 ):
344:                     pair = (min(entity.id, candidate.id), max(entity.id, candidate.id))
345:                     if pair not in seen:
346:                         seen.add(pair)
347:                         pairs.append((entity.id, candidate.id))
348:             return pairs
349: 
350:         # Fallback: O(n^2) pairwise within type groups
351:         type_groups: dict[str, list[Entity]] = {}
352:         for entity in entities:
353:             type_key = str(entity.entity_type.value if hasattr(entity.entity_type, "value") else entity.entity_type)
354:             if type_key not in type_groups:
355:                 type_groups[type_key] = []
356:             type_groups[type_key].append(entity)
357: 
358:         for group in type_groups.values():
359:             if len(group) < 2:
360:                 continue
361: 
362:             for i, e1 in enumerate(group):
363:                 for e2 in group[i + 1 :]:
364:                     similarity = levenshtein_similarity(e1.name, e2.name)
365:                     if similarity >= self._fuzzy_threshold:
366:                         pairs.append((e1.id, e2.id))
367: 
368:         return pairs
369: 
370:     def _merge_entity_group(self, entities: list[Entity]) -> Entity:
371:         """Merge a group of entities into one.
372: 
373:         Strategy:
374:         - Use the entity with highest confidence as base
375:         - Merge attributes (non-empty values preferred)
376:         - Combine source document/chunk IDs
377:         - Sum mention counts
378:         - Keep earliest created_at
379:         """
380:         if not entities:
381:             raise ValueError("Cannot merge empty entity list")
382: 
383:         if len(entities) == 1:
384:             return entities[0]
385: 
386:         # Sort by confidence (highest first)
387:         sorted_entities = sorted(entities, key=lambda e: e.confidence, reverse=True)
388:         base = sorted_entities[0]
389: 
390:         # Merge in remaining entities
391:         for entity in sorted_entities[1:]:
392:             base.merge_with(entity)
393: 
394:         return base
395: 
396:     def _update_relationships(
397:         self,
398:         relationships: list[Relationship],
399:         entity_mapping: dict[UUID, UUID],
400:     ) -> list[Relationship]:
401:         """Update relationships to use unified entity IDs."""
402:         updated = []
403:         for rel in relationships:
404:             # Create a copy with updated IDs
405:             new_source = entity_mapping.get(rel.source_entity_id, rel.source_entity_id)
406:             new_target = entity_mapping.get(rel.target_entity_id, rel.target_entity_id)
407: 
408:             # Skip self-referential relationships that emerged from merging
409:             if new_source == new_target:
410:                 continue
411: 
412:             # Update the relationship IDs
413:             rel.source_entity_id = new_source
414:             rel.target_entity_id = new_target
415:             updated.append(rel)
416: 
417:         return updated
418: 
419:     def _find_new_relationships(
420:         self,
421:         merge_groups: list[set[UUID]],
422:         entities: list[Entity],
423:     ) -> list[Relationship]:
424:         """Find new relationships to create based on correlation rules."""
425:         # This would create relationships like SAME_AS or REFERENCES
426:         # For now, return empty list - implementation depends on requirements
427:         return []
````

## File: src/khora/pipelines/flows/expansion.py
````python
  1: """Standalone semantic expansion flow for knowledge graph enhancement.
  2: 
  3: Runs semantic expansion (entity unification, relationship inference) on
  4: existing entities and relationships in a namespace.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import asyncio
 10: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 11: from uuid import UUID
 12: 
 13: from loguru import logger
 14: 
 15: from ..registry import pipeline
 16: 
 17: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 18:     from khora.core.models import Entity, Relationship
 19:     from khora.extraction.expansion import ExpansionResult
 20:     from khora.extraction.skills import ExpertiseConfig
 21:     from khora.storage import StorageCoordinator
 22: 
 23: 
 24: async def load_entities(
 25:     namespace_id: UUID,
 26:     storage: StorageCoordinator,
 27:     *,
 28:     limit: int = 1000,
 29: ) -> list[Entity]:
 30:     """Load entities from storage for expansion.
 31: 
 32:     Args:
 33:         namespace_id: Namespace to load from
 34:         storage: Storage coordinator
 35:         limit: Maximum entities to load
 36: 
 37:     Returns:
 38:         List of entities
 39:     """
 40:     # Use graph backend if available, otherwise relational
 41:     if storage.graph:
 42:         return await storage.graph.get_entities_by_namespace(namespace_id, limit=limit)
 43:     elif storage.relational:
 44:         return await storage.relational.get_entities_by_namespace(namespace_id, limit=limit)
 45:     return []
 46: 
 47: 
 48: async def load_relationships(
 49:     namespace_id: UUID,
 50:     storage: StorageCoordinator,
 51:     *,
 52:     limit: int = 5000,
 53: ) -> list[Relationship]:
 54:     """Load relationships from storage for expansion.
 55: 
 56:     Args:
 57:         namespace_id: Namespace to load from
 58:         storage: Storage coordinator
 59:         limit: Maximum relationships to load
 60: 
 61:     Returns:
 62:         List of relationships
 63:     """
 64:     if storage.graph:
 65:         return await storage.graph.get_relationships_by_namespace(namespace_id, limit=limit)
 66:     elif storage.relational:
 67:         return await storage.relational.get_relationships_by_namespace(namespace_id, limit=limit)
 68:     return []
 69: 
 70: 
 71: async def run_expansion(
 72:     entities: list[Entity],
 73:     relationships: list[Relationship],
 74:     namespace_id: UUID,
 75:     expertise: ExpertiseConfig | None = None,
 76:     *,
 77:     inference_depth: int = 2,
 78: ) -> ExpansionResult:
 79:     """Run semantic expansion on entities and relationships.
 80: 
 81:     Args:
 82:         entities: Entities to expand
 83:         relationships: Existing relationships
 84:         namespace_id: Namespace ID
 85:         expertise: Expertise configuration
 86:         inference_depth: Number of inference passes
 87: 
 88:     Returns:
 89:         Expansion result
 90:     """
 91:     from khora.extraction.expansion import SemanticExpander
 92: 
 93:     expander = SemanticExpander(
 94:         expertise=expertise,
 95:         inference_depth=inference_depth,
 96:     )
 97: 
 98:     return await expander.expand(
 99:         entities=entities,
100:         relationships=relationships,
101:         namespace_id=namespace_id,
102:     )
103: 
104: 
105: async def store_expansion_results(
106:     result: ExpansionResult,
107:     storage: StorageCoordinator,
108: ) -> dict[str, int]:
109:     """Store expansion results (merged entities, inferred relationships).
110: 
111:     Args:
112:         result: Expansion result to store
113:         storage: Storage coordinator
114: 
115:     Returns:
116:         Statistics about stored items
117:     """
118:     stored_entities = 0
119:     stored_relationships = 0
120: 
121:     # Update merged entities
122:     entity_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(20)
123: 
124:     async def update_entity(entity):
125:         nonlocal stored_entities
126:         async with entity_semaphore:
127:             await storage.update_entity(entity)
128:             stored_entities += 1
129: 
130:     if result.merged_entity_count > 0:
131:         # Only update entities that were actually modified by merges
132:         merged_ids = set(result.entity_mapping.values()) if result.entity_mapping else set()
133:         modified_entities = [e for e in result.entities if e.id in merged_ids] if merged_ids else result.entities
134:         await asyncio.gather(*[update_entity(e) for e in modified_entities])
135: 
136:     # Store inferred relationships in batch
137:     if result.inferred_relationships:
138:         stored_relationships = await storage.create_relationships_batch(result.inferred_relationships)
139: 
140:     return {
141:         "updated_entities": stored_entities,
142:         "stored_relationships": stored_relationships,
143:     }
144: 
145: 
146: @pipeline("expand_knowledge", description="Semantic expansion of knowledge graph", tags=["expansion", "enrichment"])
147: async def expand_knowledge_graph(
148:     namespace_id: UUID,
149:     storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None,
150:     *,
151:     expertise: ExpertiseConfig | str | None = None,
152:     inference_depth: int = 2,
153:     max_entities: int = 1000,
154:     max_relationships: int = 5000,
155:     store_results: bool = True,
156:     **kwargs,
157: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
158:     """Expand a namespace's knowledge graph with semantic enrichment.
159: 
160:     Runs semantic expansion on existing entities and relationships:
161:     - Cross-tool entity unification (merge duplicates)
162:     - Relationship inference (infer new relationships from patterns)
163: 
164:     Args:
165:         namespace_id: Target namespace
166:         storage: StorageCoordinator instance
167:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig, expertise name, or file path
168:         inference_depth: Number of inference passes
169:         max_entities: Maximum entities to process
170:         max_relationships: Maximum relationships to process
171:         store_results: Whether to persist results to storage
172: 
173:     Returns:
174:         Summary of expansion results
175:     """
176:     if storage is None:
177:         raise ValueError("storage is required")
178: 
179:     # Resolve expertise
180:     resolved_expertise: ExpertiseConfig | None = None
181:     if expertise is not None:
182:         from khora.extraction.skills import ExpertiseConfig as EC
183:         from khora.extraction.skills import load_expertise
184: 
185:         if isinstance(expertise, EC):
186:             resolved_expertise = expertise
187:         elif isinstance(expertise, str):
188:             try:
189:                 resolved_expertise = load_expertise(expertise)
190:             except Exception as e:
191:                 logger.warning(f"Failed to load expertise '{expertise}': {e}")
192: 
193:     logger.info(f"Starting knowledge graph expansion for namespace {namespace_id}")
194: 
195:     # Load existing data in parallel
196:     entities, relationships = await asyncio.gather(
197:         load_entities(namespace_id, storage, limit=max_entities),
198:         load_relationships(namespace_id, storage, limit=max_relationships),
199:     )
200: 
201:     logger.info(f"Loaded {len(entities)} entities and {len(relationships)} relationships")
202: 
203:     if not entities:
204:         return {
205:             "original_entities": 0,
206:             "original_relationships": 0,
207:             "unified_entities": 0,
208:             "merged_count": 0,
209:             "inferred_relationships": 0,
210:             "stored": False,
211:         }
212: 
213:     # Run expansion
214:     result = await run_expansion(
215:         entities,
216:         relationships,
217:         namespace_id,
218:         expertise=resolved_expertise,
219:         inference_depth=inference_depth,
220:     )
221: 
222:     logger.info(
223:         f"Expansion complete: {result.total_entities} entities "
224:         f"({result.merged_entity_count} merged), "
225:         f"{result.inferred_relationship_count} relationships inferred"
226:     )
227: 
228:     # Store results if requested
229:     stored = False
230:     if store_results and (result.merged_entity_count > 0 or result.inferred_relationship_count > 0):
231:         store_stats = await store_expansion_results(result, storage)
232:         stored = True
233:         logger.info(
234:             f"Stored expansion results: {store_stats['updated_entities']} entities, "
235:             f"{store_stats['stored_relationships']} relationships"
236:         )
237: 
238:     return {
239:         "original_entities": result.original_entity_count,
240:         "original_relationships": result.original_relationship_count,
241:         "unified_entities": result.total_entities,
242:         "merged_count": result.merged_entity_count,
243:         "inferred_relationships": result.inferred_relationship_count,
244:         "stored": stored,
245:     }
246: 
247: 
248: @pipeline("unify_entities", description="Cross-tool entity unification only", tags=["expansion", "unification"])
249: async def unify_entities(
250:     namespace_id: UUID,
251:     storage: StorageCoordinator | None = None,
252:     *,
253:     expertise: ExpertiseConfig | str | None = None,
254:     max_entities: int = 1000,
255:     store_results: bool = True,
256:     **kwargs,
257: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
258:     """Unify entities across tools without relationship inference.
259: 
260:     A lighter-weight expansion that only runs cross-tool entity unification
261:     to merge duplicate entities.
262: 
263:     Args:
264:         namespace_id: Target namespace
265:         storage: StorageCoordinator instance
266:         expertise: ExpertiseConfig with correlation rules
267:         max_entities: Maximum entities to process
268:         store_results: Whether to persist results to storage
269: 
270:     Returns:
271:         Summary of unification results
272:     """
273:     if storage is None:
274:         raise ValueError("storage is required")
275: 
276:     # Resolve expertise
277:     resolved_expertise: ExpertiseConfig | None = None
278:     if expertise is not None:
279:         from khora.extraction.skills import ExpertiseConfig as EC
280:         from khora.extraction.skills import load_expertise
281: 
282:         if isinstance(expertise, EC):
283:             resolved_expertise = expertise
284:         elif isinstance(expertise, str):
285:             try:
286:                 resolved_expertise = load_expertise(expertise)
287:             except Exception as e:
288:                 logger.warning(f"Failed to load expertise '{expertise}': {e}")
289: 
290:     logger.info(f"Starting entity unification for namespace {namespace_id}")
291: 
292:     # Load entities and relationships
293:     entities = await load_entities(namespace_id, storage, limit=max_entities)
294:     relationships = await load_relationships(namespace_id, storage, limit=max_entities * 5)
295: 
296:     if not entities:
297:         return {
298:             "original_entities": 0,
299:             "unified_entities": 0,
300:             "merged_count": 0,
301:             "stored": False,
302:         }
303: 
304:     # Run unification only
305:     from khora.extraction.expansion import CrossToolUnifier
306: 
307:     unifier = CrossToolUnifier(expertise=resolved_expertise)
308:     result = unifier.unify(entities, relationships)
309: 
310:     logger.info(f"Unification complete: {len(result.unified_entities)} entities " f"({result.entities_merged} merged)")
311: 
312:     # Store results if requested
313:     stored = False
314:     if store_results and result.entities_merged > 0:
315:         import asyncio
316: 
317:         entity_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(20)
318: 
319:         async def update_entity(entity):
320:             async with entity_semaphore:
321:                 await storage.update_entity(entity)
322: 
323:         await asyncio.gather(*[update_entity(e) for e in result.unified_entities])
324:         stored = True
325: 
326:     return {
327:         "original_entities": len(entities),
328:         "unified_entities": len(result.unified_entities),
329:         "merged_count": result.entities_merged,
330:         "merge_groups": len(result.merge_groups),
331:         "stored": stored,
332:     }
````

## File: src/khora/pipelines/tasks/embed.py
````python
 1: """Embedding task for chunks."""
 2: 
 3: from __future__ import annotations
 4: 
 5: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
 6: 
 7: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 8:     from khora.core.models import Chunk
 9: 
10: 
11: async def embed_chunks(
12:     chunks: list[Chunk],
13:     *,
14:     model: str = "text-embedding-3-small",
15:     batch_size: int = 100,
16:     shared_embedder: Any | None = None,
17: ) -> list[Chunk]:
18:     """Generate embeddings for chunks.
19: 
20:     Args:
21:         chunks: Chunks to embed
22:         model: Embedding model to use
23:         batch_size: Batch size for embedding
24:         shared_embedder: Optional shared embedder instance (preserves LRU cache across calls)
25: 
26:     Returns:
27:         Chunks with embeddings set
28:     """
29:     from khora.extraction.embedders import LiteLLMEmbedder
30: 
31:     if not chunks:
32:         return []
33: 
34:     # Use shared embedder if provided, otherwise create a fresh one
35:     embedder = shared_embedder or LiteLLMEmbedder(model=model, batch_size=batch_size)
36: 
37:     # Extract texts
38:     texts = [chunk.content for chunk in chunks]
39: 
40:     # Generate embeddings
41:     embeddings = await embedder.embed_batch(texts)
42: 
43:     # Update chunks with embeddings
44:     for chunk, embedding in zip(chunks, embeddings):
45:         chunk.embedding = embedding
46:         chunk.embedding_model = model
47: 
48:     return chunks
````

## File: src/khora/query/cache.py
````python
  1: """Query result cache with TTL for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Provides an in-memory LRU cache that avoids re-executing identical
  4: queries within a configurable time-to-live window.
  5: """
  6: 
  7: from __future__ import annotations
  8: 
  9: import asyncio
 10: from datetime import datetime, timedelta
 11: from hashlib import sha256
 12: from typing import Any
 13: from uuid import UUID
 14: 
 15: 
 16: class QueryCache:
 17:     """LRU cache for query results with TTL."""
 18: 
 19:     def __init__(self, max_size: int = 1000, ttl_seconds: int = 300) -> None:
 20:         """Initialize the cache.
 21: 
 22:         Args:
 23:             max_size: Maximum cached entries before eviction.
 24:             ttl_seconds: Time-to-live for each entry in seconds.
 25:         """
 26:         self._cache: dict[str, tuple[datetime, Any]] = {}
 27:         self._max_size = max_size
 28:         self._ttl = timedelta(seconds=ttl_seconds)
 29:         self._lock = asyncio.Lock()
 30:         self._hits = 0
 31:         self._misses = 0
 32: 
 33:     @staticmethod
 34:     def _make_key(query: str, namespace_id: UUID, mode: str) -> str:
 35:         return sha256(f"{query}:{namespace_id}:{mode}".encode()).hexdigest()
 36: 
 37:     async def get(self, query: str, namespace_id: UUID, mode: str) -> Any | None:
 38:         """Look up a cached result.
 39: 
 40:         Returns None on miss or expiry.
 41:         """
 42:         key = self._make_key(query, namespace_id, mode)
 43:         async with self._lock:
 44:             entry = self._cache.get(key)
 45:             if entry is not None:
 46:                 timestamp, result = entry
 47:                 if datetime.now() - timestamp < self._ttl:
 48:                     self._hits += 1
 49:                     self._record_cache_event(True, namespace_id)
 50:                     return result
 51:                 del self._cache[key]
 52:             self._misses += 1
 53:             self._record_cache_event(False, namespace_id)
 54:             return None
 55: 
 56:     @staticmethod
 57:     def _record_cache_event(hit: bool, namespace_id: UUID) -> None:
 58:         """Record a cache hit/miss to telemetry."""
 59:         from khora.telemetry import get_collector
 60: 
 61:         get_collector().record_llm_call(
 62:             operation="query_cache",
 63:             cache_hit=hit,
 64:             latency_ms=0.0,
 65:             namespace_id=namespace_id,
 66:             metadata={"cache_type": "query"},
 67:         )
 68: 
 69:     async def set(self, query: str, namespace_id: UUID, mode: str, result: Any) -> None:
 70:         """Store a result in the cache, evicting the oldest entry if full."""
 71:         key = self._make_key(query, namespace_id, mode)
 72:         async with self._lock:
 73:             if len(self._cache) >= self._max_size:
 74:                 oldest_key = min(self._cache, key=lambda k: self._cache[k][0])
 75:                 del self._cache[oldest_key]
 76:             self._cache[key] = (datetime.now(), result)
 77: 
 78:     async def invalidate(self, namespace_id: UUID | None = None) -> int:
 79:         """Remove entries, optionally filtered by namespace.
 80: 
 81:         Args:
 82:             namespace_id: If provided, only remove entries whose key
 83:                           was generated with this namespace. If None,
 84:                           clears the entire cache.
 85: 
 86:         Returns:
 87:             Number of entries removed.
 88:         """
 89:         async with self._lock:
 90:             if namespace_id is None:
 91:                 count = len(self._cache)
 92:                 self._cache.clear()
 93:                 return count
 94:             # Namespace is hashed into the key, so we can't filter efficiently.
 95:             # Clear everything as a safe fallback.
 96:             count = len(self._cache)
 97:             self._cache.clear()
 98:             return count
 99: 
100:     @property
101:     def stats(self) -> dict[str, int]:
102:         """Return hit/miss statistics."""
103:         return {
104:             "size": len(self._cache),
105:             "hits": self._hits,
106:             "misses": self._misses,
107:         }
````

## File: src/khora/query/hyde.py
````python
 1: """Hypothetical Document Embeddings (HyDE) for query expansion.
 2: 
 3: Generates a hypothetical answer document using an LLM, embeds it,
 4: and averages with the original query embedding to improve recall
 5: for queries that use different vocabulary than stored documents.
 6: """
 7: 
 8: from __future__ import annotations
 9: 
10: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
11: 
12: import numpy as np
13: from loguru import logger
14: 
15: if TYPE_CHECKING:
16:     from khora.config.llm import LiteLLMConfig
17:     from khora.extraction.embedders import Embedder
18: 
19: 
20: class HyDEExpander:
21:     """Expands query embeddings using Hypothetical Document Embeddings."""
22: 
23:     def __init__(
24:         self,
25:         embedder: Embedder,
26:         llm_config: LiteLLMConfig | None = None,
27:         *,
28:         num_hypotheticals: int = 1,
29:     ) -> None:
30:         self._embedder = embedder
31:         self._llm_config = llm_config
32:         self._num_hypotheticals = num_hypotheticals
33: 
34:     async def generate_hypothetical(self, query: str) -> str:
35:         """Generate a hypothetical document that answers the query."""
36:         from khora.config.llm import acompletion
37: 
38:         system_prompt = (
39:             "You are a knowledgeable assistant. Given a question, write a short, "
40:             "factual passage (2-3 sentences) that directly answers it. "
41:             "Do not include any preamble or meta-commentary, just the answer content."
42:         )
43: 
44:         return await acompletion(
45:             query,
46:             self._llm_config,
47:             system_prompt=system_prompt,
48:             temperature=0.7,
49:             max_tokens=200,
50:         )
51: 
52:     async def expand_query_embedding(
53:         self,
54:         query: str,
55:         query_embedding: list[float],
56:     ) -> list[float]:
57:         """Expand a query embedding by averaging with hypothetical doc embeddings.
58: 
59:         On any failure, returns the original embedding unchanged.
60:         """
61:         from khora.telemetry.instrument import pipeline_stage
62: 
63:         try:
64:             async with pipeline_stage("query", "hyde", extra_metadata={"num_hypotheticals": self._num_hypotheticals}):
65:                 hypotheticals: list[str] = []
66:                 for _ in range(self._num_hypotheticals):
67:                     doc = await self.generate_hypothetical(query)
68:                     hypotheticals.append(doc)
69: 
70:                 # Embed hypothetical documents
71:                 hyde_embeddings: list[list[float]] = []
72:                 for doc in hypotheticals:
73:                     emb = await self._embedder.embed(doc)
74:                     hyde_embeddings.append(emb)
75: 
76:                 # Average: original query embedding + hypothetical embeddings
77:                 all_embeddings = [query_embedding, *hyde_embeddings]
78:                 avg = np.mean(all_embeddings, axis=0)
79:                 return avg.tolist()
80: 
81:         except Exception as e:
82:             logger.warning(f"HyDE expansion failed, using original embedding: {e}")
83:             return query_embedding
````

## File: src/khora/query/reranking.py
````python
  1: """Reranking module for Khora Memory Lake.
  2: 
  3: Provides neural re-ranking of search results using:
  4: - Cross-encoder models (sentence-transformers)
  5: - LLM-based relevance scoring
  6: """
  7: 
  8: from __future__ import annotations
  9: 
 10: from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
 11: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
 12: from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Generic, TypeVar
 13: 
 14: from loguru import logger
 15: 
 16: from khora.core.models.entity import entity_type_str
 17: 
 18: if TYPE_CHECKING:
 19:     from khora.config.llm import LiteLLMConfig
 20:     from khora.core.models import Chunk, Entity
 21: 
 22: T = TypeVar("T")
 23: 
 24: 
 25: @dataclass
 26: class RerankCandidate(Generic[T]):
 27:     """A candidate for reranking."""
 28: 
 29:     item: T
 30:     original_score: float
 31:     content: str  # Text content for reranking
 32:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 33: 
 34: 
 35: @dataclass
 36: class RerankResult(Generic[T]):
 37:     """Result of reranking."""
 38: 
 39:     item: T
 40:     original_score: float
 41:     rerank_score: float
 42:     final_score: float
 43:     metadata: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
 44: 
 45: 
 46: class Reranker(ABC):
 47:     """Abstract base class for rerankers."""
 48: 
 49:     @abstractmethod
 50:     async def rerank(
 51:         self,
 52:         query: str,
 53:         candidates: list[RerankCandidate[T]],
 54:         top_k: int = 10,
 55:     ) -> list[RerankResult[T]]:
 56:         """Rerank candidates based on relevance to query.
 57: 
 58:         Args:
 59:             query: Query text
 60:             candidates: Candidates to rerank
 61:             top_k: Number of results to return
 62: 
 63:         Returns:
 64:             List of RerankResult sorted by final_score descending
 65:         """
 66:         pass
 67: 
 68: 
 69: class CrossEncoderReranker(Reranker):
 70:     """Reranker using cross-encoder models.
 71: 
 72:     Uses sentence-transformers cross-encoder models for
 73:     high-quality relevance scoring.
 74:     """
 75: 
 76:     def __init__(
 77:         self,
 78:         model_name: str = "cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2",
 79:         device: str | None = None,
 80:         batch_size: int = 32,
 81:     ) -> None:
 82:         """Initialize the cross-encoder reranker.
 83: 
 84:         Args:
 85:             model_name: Cross-encoder model name from sentence-transformers
 86:             device: Device to use (cuda, cpu, or None for auto)
 87:             batch_size: Batch size for scoring
 88:         """
 89:         self._model_name = model_name
 90:         self._device = device
 91:         self._batch_size = batch_size
 92:         self._model = None
 93: 
 94:     def _get_model(self):
 95:         """Lazy load the cross-encoder model."""
 96:         if self._model is None:
 97:             try:
 98:                 from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder
 99:             except ImportError:
100:                 raise RuntimeError("sentence-transformers not installed. " "Run: pip install sentence-transformers")
101:             self._model = CrossEncoder(self._model_name, device=self._device)
102:         return self._model
103: 
104:     async def rerank(
105:         self,
106:         query: str,
107:         candidates: list[RerankCandidate[T]],
108:         top_k: int = 10,
109:     ) -> list[RerankResult[T]]:
110:         """Rerank using cross-encoder scoring.
111: 
112:         Args:
113:             query: Query text
114:             candidates: Candidates to rerank
115:             top_k: Number of results to return
116: 
117:         Returns:
118:             Reranked results
119:         """
120:         if not candidates:
121:             return []
122: 
123:         try:
124:             model = self._get_model()
125: 
126:             # Prepare pairs for cross-encoder
127:             pairs = [(query, c.content) for c in candidates]
128: 
129:             # Score in batches
130:             scores = model.predict(pairs, batch_size=self._batch_size)
131: 
132:             # Combine with original scores
133:             results = []
134:             for candidate, rerank_score in zip(candidates, scores):
135:                 # Normalize rerank score to 0-1
136:                 normalized_score = (
137:                     float(1 / (1 + (-rerank_score).exp())) if hasattr(rerank_score, "exp") else float(rerank_score)
138:                 )
139: 
140:                 # Combine scores (weighted average)
141:                 final_score = 0.7 * normalized_score + 0.3 * candidate.original_score
142: 
143:                 results.append(
144:                     RerankResult(
145:                         item=candidate.item,
146:                         original_score=candidate.original_score,
147:                         rerank_score=normalized_score,
148:                         final_score=final_score,
149:                         metadata=candidate.metadata,
150:                     )
151:                 )
152: 
153:             # Sort by final score
154:             results.sort(key=lambda r: r.final_score, reverse=True)
155:             return results[:top_k]
156: 
157:         except Exception as e:
158:             logger.warning(f"Cross-encoder reranking failed: {e}")
159:             # Fall back to original ranking
160:             return [
161:                 RerankResult(
162:                     item=c.item,
163:                     original_score=c.original_score,
164:                     rerank_score=c.original_score,
165:                     final_score=c.original_score,
166:                     metadata=c.metadata,
167:                 )
168:                 for c in sorted(candidates, key=lambda x: x.original_score, reverse=True)[:top_k]
169:             ]
170: 
171: 
172: LLM_RERANK_PROMPT = """You are a relevance scoring system. Given a query and a document, score the relevance of the document to the query.
173: 
174: Query: {query}
175: 
176: Document:
177: {document}
178: 
179: Score the relevance from 0 to 10 where:
180: - 0: Completely irrelevant
181: - 5: Somewhat relevant, mentions related topics
182: - 10: Highly relevant, directly answers or addresses the query
183: 
184: Respond with ONLY a single number (0-10), nothing else."""
185: 
186: LLM_BATCH_RERANK_PROMPT = """You are a relevance scoring system. Given a query and multiple passages, score the relevance of each passage to the query.
187: 
188: Query: {query}
189: 
190: Passages:
191: {passages}
192: 
193: Score each passage from 0 to 10 where:
194: - 0: Completely irrelevant
195: - 5: Somewhat relevant, mentions related topics
196: - 10: Highly relevant, directly answers or addresses the query
197: 
198: Respond with ONLY a JSON object: {{"scores": [score1, score2, ...]}}
199: The scores array must have exactly {count} numbers, one per passage in order."""
200: 
201: 
202: class LLMReranker(Reranker):
203:     """Reranker using LLM-based relevance scoring.
204:
````
